  "text": "## Summary of Envoy Access Logging Infrastructure\n\n### C++ Source Files\n\n#### Access Log Filter Implementations\n\nFilters control *whether* a log entry is emitted; they do not define the sink/format.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/common/access_log/access_log_impl.h` + `.cc`**\nDefines all core built-in access log filter classes:\n- `FilterFactory` – reads filter config from proto and instantiates filters\n- `AccessLogFactory` – instantiates access log sink instances from proto\n- `StatusCodeFilter` – filters by HTTP response status code\n- `DurationFilter` – filters by request/response duration\n- `AndFilter` / `OrFilter` – logical operator composition filters\n- `NotHealthCheckFilter` – filters out health check requests\n- `TraceableRequestFilter` – passes only traceable requests\n- `RuntimeFilter` – probabilistic filter driven by a runtime key\n- `HeaderFilter` – filters by request/response header value\n- `ResponseFlagFilter` – filters by Envoy response flags\n- `GrpcStatusFilter` – filters by gRPC status code\n- `LogTypeFilter` – filters by access log type (TCP, HTTP, etc.)\n- `MetadataFilter` – filters by dynamic metadata value\n\nThese are purely decision filters; JSON vs. text is irrelevant here.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/cel.h` + `.cc`**\n- `CELAccessLogExtensionFilter` (extension name: `envoy.access_loggers.extension_filters.cel`) – evaluates a CEL expression to decide whether to emit a log. Also a filter, not a sink.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/config.h` + `.cc`**\nFactory that instantiates the CEL filter.\n\n#### Access Log Sink Implementations\n\nSinks define *where* and *how* log entries are written.\n\n**1. File sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.file`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/file_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLog` class (writes formatted strings to a file/fd)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/file/config.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLogFactory`\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text logging.** The `FileAccessLog` config proto (`file/v3/file.proto`) has four format options: `format` (plain text string, deprecated), `json_format` (struct/JSON, deprecated), `typed_json_format` (typed JSON, deprecated), and `log_format` (a `SubstitutionFormatString` which itself supports both text format and JSON format). The factory explicitly handles all four cases including `createJsonFormatter`.\n\n**2. Stream sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.stdout`, `envoy.access_loggers.stderr`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/stream/config.h` / `.cc` – `StdoutAccessLogFactory`, `StderrAccessLogFactory`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/stream_access_log_common_impl.h` – template helper `createStreamAccessLogInstance`\n- These reuse `FileAccessLog` but write to Stdout/Stderr destinations.\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text** via `SubstitutionFormatString` (proto: `stream/v3/stream.proto`).\n\n**3. gRPC (ALS) sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.http_grpc`, `envoy.access_loggers.tcp_grpc`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `GrpcAccessLoggerImpl`, `GrpcAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `HttpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/tcp_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `TcpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_config.h` / `.cc`, `tcp_config.h` / `.cc` – factory classes\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_utils.h` / `.cc`, `grpc_access_log_proto_descriptors.h` / `.cc`, `config_utils.h` / `.cc` – utilities and proto descriptors\n- **Does NOT support JSON or plain text output.** Logs are serialized as typed protobuf messages (`envoy.data.accesslog.v3.HTTPAccessLogEntry` / `TCPAccessLogEntry`) and streamed over gRPC to an ALS server. This is structured protobuf-binary transport, not JSON.\n\n**4. OpenTelemetry sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.open_telemetry`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `AccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `GrpcAccessLoggerImpl`, `GrpcAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/config.h` / `.cc` – factory\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/substitution_formatter.h` / `.cc` – `OpenTelemetryFormatter`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/access_log_proto_descriptors.h` / `.cc`\n- **Does NOT support unstructured text.** Logs are serialized as OTLP `LogRecord` protobuf messages (sent via gRPC or HTTP to an OpenTelemetry collector). The body and attributes are structured OTel key-value pairs. The formatter uses substitution expressions to populate fields, but the wire format is structured OTLP, not raw text or JSON.\n\n**5. Fluentd sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.fluentd`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/fluentd_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdAccessLog`, `FluentdAccessLoggerImpl`, `FluentdAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/config.h` / `.cc` – factory\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/substitution_formatter.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdFormatter` (msgpack serializer)\n- **Supports structured logging only (msgpack format).** Logs are encoded in the Fluentd Forward Protocol (msgpack-encoded key-value records) and streamed over TCP. The record is a protobuf Struct with access log command operators as values. This is structured, NOT plain text.\n\n**6. Wasm sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.wasm`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/wasm/wasm_access_log_impl.h` – `WasmAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/wasm/config.h` / `.cc` – `WasmAccessLogFactory`\n- **Format determined by the WASM module.** The host passes request context and stream info to the loaded WASM plugin, which implements its own logging logic. It can be structured or unstructured depending on the module implementation.\n\n#### Common Infrastructure\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/access_log_base.h` / `.cc` – `ImplBase`: base class that calls `filter_->evaluate()` then `emitLog()`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger.h` – template interface `GrpcAccessLogger<HttpLogProto, TcpLogProto>` and `GrpcAccessLoggerCache`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger_clients.h` – gRPC client wrappers\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger_utils.h` / `.cc` – utilities for gRPC loggers\n\n### Proto Definitions (`data-plane-api--84e84367`)\n\nAll proto definitions live under `envoy/extensions/access_loggers/`:\n\n| Proto file | Message(s) | Sink/Filter | JSON support |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `file/v3/file.proto` | `FileAccessLog` | Sink | YES – `json_format`, `typed_json_format`, `log_format` (SubstitutionFormatString with json_format) |\n| `stream/v3/stream.proto` | `StdoutAccessLog`, `StderrAccessLog` | Sink | YES – via `SubstitutionFormatString log_format` |\n| `grpc/v3/als.proto` | `HttpGrpcAccessLogConfig`, `TcpGrpcAccessLogConfig`, `CommonGrpcAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured protobuf-binary over gRPC only |\n| `open_telemetry/v3/logs_service.proto` | `OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured OTLP protobuf (gRPC or HTTP) only |\n| `fluentd/v3/fluentd.proto` | `FluentdAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured msgpack (Fluentd Forward Protocol) only |\n| `wasm/v3/wasm.proto` | `WasmAccessLog` | Sink | Module-dependent |\n| `filters/cel/v3/cel.proto` | `ExpressionFilter` | Filter | N/A (filter, not a sink) |\n| `filters/process_ratelimit/v3/process_ratelimit.proto` | `ProcessRateLimitFilter` | Filter | N/A (filter, not a sink) |\n| `stats/v3/stats.proto` | `Config` | Sink (stats) | NO – emits Envoy stats counters/histograms, not log text |\n| `dynamic_modules/v3/dynamic_modules.proto` | `DynamicModuleAccessLog` | Sink | Module-dependent (config can pass JSON via `google.protobuf.Struct`) |\n\n### Summary\n\n- **Structured (JSON) logging**: `file` and `stream` (stdout/stderr) sinks support JSON output via `SubstitutionFormatString` with `json_format`. The `file` sink also has legacy `json_format` and `typed_json_format` fields.\n- **Unstructured text only**: None of the sinks are exclusively text-only; the `file` and `stream` sinks can do both text and JSON.\n- **Structured protobuf/binary only** (no JSON text output): `grpc` (ALS), `open_telemetry`, `fluentd`, `stats` sinks.\n- **Filters** (not sinks): built-in filters in `source/common/access_log/access_log_impl.h` and the CEL extension filter in `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/cel.h` – these control log emission, not format."
}

    18→// [#protodoc-title: Dynamic Modules Access Logger]
    19→// [#extension: envoy.access_loggers.dynamic_modules]
    20→
    21→// Configuration for the Dynamic Modules Access Logger. This logger allows loading shared object
    22→// files via ``dlopen`` to implement custom access logging behavior.
    23→//
    24→// A module can be loaded by multiple access loggers; the module is loaded only once and shared
    25→// across multiple logger instances.
    26→//
    27→// The access logger receives completed request information including request/response headers,
    28→// stream info (timing, upstream info, response codes), and the log context type.
    29→message DynamicModuleAccessLog {
    30→  // Specifies the shared-object level configuration. This field is required.
    31→  envoy.extensions.dynamic_modules.v3.DynamicModuleConfig dynamic_module_config = 1
    32→      [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];
    33→
    34→  // The name for this logger configuration. If not specified, defaults to an empty string.
    35→  //
    36→  // This can be used to distinguish between different logger implementations inside a dynamic
    37→  // module. For example, a module can have completely different logger implementations (e.g.,
    38→  // file logger, gRPC logger, metrics logger). When Envoy receives this configuration, it passes
    39→  // the ``logger_name`` to the dynamic module's access logger config init function together with
    40→  // the ``logger_config``. That way a module can decide which in-module logger implementation to
    41→  // use based on the name at load time.
    42→  string logger_name = 2;
    43→
    44→  // The configuration for the logger chosen by ``logger_name``. If not specified, an empty
    45→  // configuration is passed to the module.
    46→  //
    47→  // This is passed to the module's access logger initialization function. Together with the
    48→  // ``logger_name``, the module can decide which in-module logger implementation to use and
    49→  // fine-tune the behavior of the logger.
    50→  //
    51→  // For example, if a module has two logger implementations, one for file output and one for
    52→  // sending to an external service, ``logger_name`` is used to choose either file or external.
    53→  // The ``logger_config`` can be used to configure file paths, service endpoints, batching
    54→  // parameters, format strings, etc.
    55→  //
    56→  // ``google.protobuf.Struct`` is serialized as JSON before passing it to the module.
    57→  // ``google.protobuf.BytesValue`` and ``google.protobuf.StringValue`` are passed directly
    58→  // without the wrapper.
    59→  //
    60→  // .. code-block:: yaml
    61→  //
    62→  //  # Passing a JSON struct configuration
    63→  //  logger_config:
    64→  //    "@type": "type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.Struct"
    65→  //    value:
    66→  //      output_path: "/var/log/envoy/access.log"
    67→  //      format: "json"
    68→  //      buffer_size: 1000
    69→  //
    70→  //  # Passing a simple string configuration
    71→  //  logger_config:
    72→  //    "@type": "type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.StringValue"
    73→  //    value: "/var/log/envoy/access.log"
    74→  //
    75→  google.protobuf.Any logger_config = 3;
    76→}

This logger allows loading shared object\n// files via ``dlopen`` to implement custom access logging behavior.\n//\n// A module can be loaded by multiple access loggers; the module is loaded only once and shared\n// across multiple logger instances.\n//\n// The access logger receives completed request information including request/response headers,\n// stream info (timing, upstream info, response codes), and the log context type.\nmessage DynamicModuleAccessLog {\n  // Specifies the shared-object level configuration. This field is required.\n  envoy.extensions.dynamic_modules.v3.DynamicModuleConfig dynamic_module_config = 1\n      [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n\n  // The name for this logger configuration. If not specified, defaults to an empty string.\n  //\n  // This can be used to distinguish between different logger implementations inside a dynamic\n  // module. For example, a module can have completely different logger implementations (e.g.,\n  // file logger, gRPC logger, metrics logger). When Envoy receives this configuration, it passes\n  // the ``logger_name`` to the dynamic module's access logger config init function together with\n  // the ``logger_config``. That way a module can decide which in-module logger implementation to\n  // use based on the name at load time.\n  string logger_name = 2;\n\n  // The configuration for the logger chosen by ``logger_name``. If not specified, an empty\n  // configuration is passed to the module.\n  //\n  // This is passed to the module's access logger initialization function. Together with the\n  // ``logger_name``, the module can decide which in-module logger implementation to use and\n  // fine-tune the behavior of the logger.\n  //\n  // For example, if a module has two logger implementations, one for file output and one for\n  // sending to an external service, ``logger_name`` is used to choose either file or external.\n  // The ``logger_config`` can be used to configure file paths, service endpoints, batching\n  // parameters, format strings, etc.\n  //\n  // ``google.protobuf.Struct`` is serialized as JSON before passing it to the module.\n  // ``google.protobuf.BytesValue`` and ``google.protobuf.StringValue`` are passed directly\n  // without the wrapper.\n  //\n  // .. code-block:: yaml\n  //\n  //  # Passing a JSON struct configuration\n  //  logger_config:\n  //    \"@type\": \"type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.Struct\"\n  //    value:\n  //      output_path: \"/var/log/envoy/access.log\"\n  //      format: \"json\"\n  //      buffer_size: 1000\n  //\n  //  # Passing a simple string configuration\n  //  logger_config:\n  //    \"@type\": \"type.googleapis.com/google.protobuf.StringValue\"\n  //    value: \"/var/log/envoy/access.log\"\n  //\n  google.protobuf.Any logger_config = 3;\n}\n", "numLines": 77, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 77}}

  "text": "## Summary of Envoy Access Logging Infrastructure\n\n### C++ Source Files\n\n#### Access Log Filter Implementations\n\nFilters control *whether* a log entry is emitted; they do not define the sink/format.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/common/access_log/access_log_impl.h` + `.cc`**\nDefines all core built-in access log filter classes:\n- `FilterFactory` – reads filter config from proto and instantiates filters\n- `AccessLogFactory` – instantiates access log sink instances from proto\n- `StatusCodeFilter` – filters by HTTP response status code\n- `DurationFilter` – filters by request/response duration\n- `AndFilter` / `OrFilter` – logical operator composition filters\n- `NotHealthCheckFilter` – filters out health check requests\n- `TraceableRequestFilter` – passes only traceable requests\n- `RuntimeFilter` – probabilistic filter driven by a runtime key\n- `HeaderFilter` – filters by request/response header value\n- `ResponseFlagFilter` – filters by Envoy response flags\n- `GrpcStatusFilter` – filters by gRPC status code\n- `LogTypeFilter` – filters by access log type (TCP, HTTP, etc.)\n- `MetadataFilter` – filters by dynamic metadata value\n\nThese are purely decision filters; JSON vs. text is irrelevant here.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/cel.h` + `.cc`**\n- `CELAccessLogExtensionFilter` (extension name: `envoy.access_loggers.extension_filters.cel`) – evaluates a CEL expression to decide whether to emit a log. Also a filter, not a sink.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/config.h` + `.cc`**\nFactory that instantiates the CEL filter.\n\n#### Access Log Sink Implementations\n\nSinks define *where* and *how* log entries are written.\n\n**1. File sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.file`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/file_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLog` class (writes formatted strings to a file/fd)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/file/config.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLogFactory`\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text logging.** The `FileAccessLog` config proto (`file/v3/file.proto`) has four format options: `format` (plain text string, deprecated), `json_format` (struct/JSON, deprecated), `typed_json_format` (typed JSON, deprecated), and `log_format` (a `SubstitutionFormatString` which itself supports both text format and JSON format). The factory explicitly handles all four cases including `createJsonFormatter`.\n\n**2. Stream sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.stdout`, `envoy.access_loggers.stderr`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/stream/config.h` / `.cc` – `StdoutAccessLogFactory`, `StderrAccessLogFactory`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/stream_access_log_common_impl.h` – template helper `createStreamAccessLogInstance`\n- These reuse `FileAccessLog` but write to Stdout/Stderr destinations.\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text** via `SubstitutionFormatString` (proto: `stream/v3/stream.proto`).\n\n**3. gRPC (ALS) sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.http_grpc`, `envoy.access_loggers.tcp_grpc`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `GrpcAccessLoggerImpl`, `GrpcAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `HttpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/tcp_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `TcpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_config.h` / `.cc`, `tcp_config.h` / `.cc` – factory classes\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_utils.h` / `.cc`, `grpc_access_log_proto_descriptors.h` / `.cc`, `config_utils.h` / `.cc` – utilities and proto descriptors\n- **Does NOT support JSON or plain text output.** Logs are serialized as typed protobuf messages (`envoy.data.accesslog.v3.HTTPAccessLogEntry` / `TCPAccessLogEntry`) and streamed over gRPC to an ALS server. This is structured protobuf-binary transport, not JSON.\n\n**4. OpenTelemetry sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.open_telemetry`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `AccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `GrpcAccessLoggerImpl`, `GrpcAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/config.h` / `.cc` – factory\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/substitution_formatter.h` / `.cc` – `OpenTelemetryFormatter`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/open_telemetry/access_log_proto_descriptors.h` / `.cc`\n- **Does NOT support unstructured text.** Logs are serialized as OTLP `LogRecord` protobuf messages (sent via gRPC or HTTP to an OpenTelemetry collector). The body and attributes are structured OTel key-value pairs. The formatter uses substitution expressions to populate fields, but the wire format is structured OTLP, not raw text or JSON.\n\n**5. Fluentd sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.fluentd`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/fluentd_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdAccessLog`, `FluentdAccessLoggerImpl`, `FluentdAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/config.h` / `.cc` – factory\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/substitution_formatter.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdFormatter` (msgpack serializer)\n- **Supports structured logging only (msgpack format).** Logs are encoded in the Fluentd Forward Protocol (msgpack-encoded key-value records) and streamed over TCP. The record is a protobuf Struct with access log command operators as values. This is structured, NOT plain text.\n\n**6. Wasm sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.wasm`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/wasm/wasm_access_log_impl.h` – `WasmAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/wasm/config.h` / `.cc` – `WasmAccessLogFactory`\n- **Format determined by the WASM module.** The host passes request context and stream info to the loaded WASM plugin, which implements its own logging logic. It can be structured or unstructured depending on the module implementation.\n\n#### Common Infrastructure\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/access_log_base.h` / `.cc` – `ImplBase`: base class that calls `filter_->evaluate()` then `emitLog()`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger.h` – template interface `GrpcAccessLogger<HttpLogProto, TcpLogProto>` and `GrpcAccessLoggerCache`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger_clients.h` – gRPC client wrappers\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/grpc_access_logger_utils.h` / `.cc` – utilities for gRPC loggers\n\n### Proto Definitions (`data-plane-api--84e84367`)\n\nAll proto definitions live under `envoy/extensions/access_loggers/`:\n\n| Proto file | Message(s) | Sink/Filter | JSON support |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `file/v3/file.proto` | `FileAccessLog` | Sink | YES – `json_format`, `typed_json_format`, `log_format` (SubstitutionFormatString with json_format) |\n| `stream/v3/stream.proto` | `StdoutAccessLog`, `StderrAccessLog` | Sink | YES – via `SubstitutionFormatString log_format` |\n| `grpc/v3/als.proto` | `HttpGrpcAccessLogConfig`, `TcpGrpcAccessLogConfig`, `CommonGrpcAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured protobuf-binary over gRPC only |\n| `open_telemetry/v3/logs_service.proto` | `OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured OTLP protobuf (gRPC or HTTP) only |\n| `fluentd/v3/fluentd.proto` | `FluentdAccessLogConfig` | Sink | NO – structured msgpack (Fluentd Forward Protocol) only |\n| `wasm/v3/wasm.proto` | `WasmAccessLog` | Sink | Filter | N/A (filter, not a sink) |\n| `stats/v3/stats.proto` | `Config` | Sink (stats) | NO – emits Envoy stats counters/histograms, not log text |\n| `dynamic_modules/v3/dynamic_modules.proto` |   "text": "## Summary of Envoy Access Logging Infrastructure\n\n### C++ Source Files\n\n#### Access Log Filter Implementations\n\nFilters control *whether* a log entry is emitted; they do not define the sink/format.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/common/access_log/access_log_impl.h` + `.cc`**\nDefines all core built-in access log filter classes:\n- `FilterFactory` – reads filter config from proto and instantiates filters\n- `AccessLogFactory` – instantiates access log sink instances from proto\n- `StatusCodeFilter` – filters by HTTP response status code\n- `DurationFilter` – filters by request/response duration\n- `AndFilter` / `OrFilter` – logical operator composition filters\n- `NotHealthCheckFilter` – filters out health check requests\n- `TraceableRequestFilter` – passes only traceable requests\n- `RuntimeFilter` – probabilistic filter driven by a runtime key\n- `HeaderFilter` – filters by request/response header value\n- `ResponseFlagFilter` – filters by Envoy response flags\n- `GrpcStatusFilter` – filters by gRPC status code\n- `LogTypeFilter` – filters by access log type (TCP, HTTP, etc.)\n- `MetadataFilter` – filters by dynamic metadata value\n\nThese are purely decision filters; JSON vs. text is irrelevant here.\n\n**`envoy--v1.31.2` / `source/extensions/access_loggers/filters/cel/cel.h` + `.cc`**\n- `CELAccessLogExtensionFilter` (extension name: `envoy.access_loggers.extension_filters.cel`) – evaluates a CEL expression to decide whether to emit a log. File sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.file`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/file_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLog` class (writes formatted strings to a file/fd)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/file/config.h` / `.cc` – `FileAccessLogFactory`\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text logging.** The `FileAccessLog` config proto (`file/v3/file.proto`) has four format options: `format` (plain text string, deprecated), `json_format` (struct/JSON, deprecated), `typed_json_format` (typed JSON, deprecated), and `log_format` (a `SubstitutionFormatString` which itself supports both text format and JSON format). Stream sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.stdout`, `envoy.access_loggers.stderr`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/stream/config.h` / `.cc` – `StdoutAccessLogFactory`, `StderrAccessLogFactory`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/common/stream_access_log_common_impl.h` – template helper `createStreamAccessLogInstance`\n- These reuse `FileAccessLog` but write to Stdout/Stderr destinations.\n- **Supports structured (JSON) AND unstructured text** via `SubstitutionFormatString` (proto: `stream/v3/stream.proto`).\n\n**3. gRPC (ALS) sinks** (`envoy.access_loggers.http_grpc`, `envoy.access_loggers.tcp_grpc`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `GrpcAccessLoggerImpl`, `GrpcAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `HttpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/tcp_grpc_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `TcpGrpcAccessLog`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/http_config.h` / `.cc`, `tcp_config.h` / `.cc` – factory classes\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/grpc/grpc_access_log_utils.h` / `.cc`, `grpc_access_log_proto_descriptors.h` / `.cc`, `config_utils.h` / `.cc` – utilities and proto descriptors\n- **Does NOT support JSON or plain text output.** Logs are serialized as typed protobuf messages (`envoy.data.accesslog.v3.HTTPAccessLogEntry` / `TCPAccessLogEntry`) and streamed over gRPC to an ALS server. The body and attributes are structured OTel key-value pairs. The formatter uses substitution expressions to populate fields, but the wire format is structured OTLP, not raw text or JSON.\n\n**5. Fluentd sink** (`envoy.access_loggers.fluentd`)\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/fluentd_access_log_impl.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdAccessLog`, `FluentdAccessLoggerImpl`, `FluentdAccessLoggerCacheImpl`\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/config.h` / `.cc` – factory\n- `source/extensions/access_loggers/fluentd/substitution_formatter.h` / `.cc` – `FluentdFormatter` (msgpack serializer)\n- **Supports structured logging only (msgpack format).** Logs are encoded in the Fluentd Forward Protocol (msgpack-encoded key-value records) and streamed over TCP. Module-dependent |\n| `filters/cel/v3/cel.proto` | `ExpressionFilter` | Filter | N/A (filter, not a sink) |\n| `filters/process_ratelimit/v3/process_ratelimit.proto` | `ProcessRateLimitFilter` |
